description_long: This is a robust, double-headed axe featuring a long, cylindrical wooden handle with a dark, capped end. The axe head is forged from dark metal and includes a broad, angular blade on one side and a sharply pointed pick on the opposite side, making it suitable for both chopping and piercing tasks. The blade has a slightly curved edge, while the pick is straight and tapers to a fine point. The head is securely mounted to the handle with visible metal fittings, suggesting durability and strength. The handle appears smooth with a natural wood grain, and the metal parts have a matte, slightly textured finish. This tool is designed for heavy-duty use, likely in construction, demolition, or emergency urban scenarios.
description: A double-headed axe with a wooden handle and a dark metal head, featuring a broad blade and a pointed pick. The design is sturdy and utilitarian, suitable for demanding urban tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, angular blade with the pointed pick opposite.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the capped end of the handle and the alignment of the blade and pick.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the slim profile of the axe head and the straightness of the handle.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the blade edge and the sharpness of the pick, with the handle receding behind.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a dark rubberized end cap, smooth surface, and round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed with a broad, angular blade on one side and a straight, pointed pick on the other; matte finish, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and pinned mounting with visible metal fittings securing the head to the handle.
